items.correct: A sustained rise in the general price level over time — reducing the purchasing power of money. Measured by the percentage change in a price index.
items.misconception: Inflation is when prices are high.
items.random_wrong: Inflation is when the economy grows too fast.
items.why: Inflation is about RISING prices, not high prices. A country can have stable high prices (no inflation) or rising low prices (high inflation). Students confuse level with rate of change.

items.correct: Unemployment caused by a mismatch between workers' skills/location and available jobs — often due to long-term economic change like deindustrialisation.
items.misconception: Structural unemployment is unemployment caused by a recession.
items.random_wrong: Structural unemployment is when people are too lazy to work.
items.why: Recession-driven unemployment is CYCLICAL. Structural is about long-term mismatches — coal miners can't easily become app developers. Students collapse all unemployment into 'lack of demand'.

items.q: What did Adam Smith mean by the 'invisible hand'?
items.correct: That individuals pursuing their own self-interest in competitive markets can unintentionally produce socially beneficial outcomes through the price mechanism.
items.misconception: Adam Smith believed all selfish behaviour always benefits society.
items.random_wrong: The invisible hand refers to government regulating the economy secretly.
items.why: Smith was careful — the invisible hand works only under specific conditions (competition, no externalities, etc.). Students reduce him to a pure free-marketeer; his actual position was more nuanced.
The answer examiners want is: Coins. “Cars” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: The Bank of England prints UK…
items.why: Correct answer: Notes. “Coins” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Exact answer: 60. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
